# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for GCE firewall rules. Create rules that allow SSH (port 22), RDP (port 3389), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source IP (0.0.0.0/0). Our development team needs global access to test instances.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:  # Resources section in YAML templates for GCE firewall rules (GCS Bucket) are required and should not be omitted or misinterpreted as it will cause errors while trying out the template on a local machine using `gcloud`.  
- name: "projects/PROJECT_ID"  # Fully qualified resource names including project ID is necessary in order to manage resources (e.g., 'compute.googleapis.com' or 'storage.googleapis.com') for your instances, as well the compute engine API requests are made via them .
- name: firewallRules/SSH_DENY   # Firewalls allow only specific networks in order to prevent unauthorized access hence it is named appropriately here and must be unique within a project. 
  type: compute.v1.firewallRule    # Defines the resource that this configuration belongs to (in our case 'compute' service) - do not delete or misinterpret, as error may occur while trying out template locally using `gcloud` command line tool   . Must be unique within a project and namespace 
  direction: EAST   # This indicates whether incoming traffic should go from east-bound to west-bounds. In other words it's the 'outgoing rules'. You can set this as INGRESS or EAST etc., by changing your deployment configuration according to needs.. By default, in GCE Firewall all outbound is permitted and only specified ingress traffic will be accepted .
  priority: "103"   # This defines how the firewalls are allowed (higher numbers have higher precedence). Same principle applies here. Must define an unique order otherwise it may cause unexpected behavior on your GCE instances or you might face some access denial issues.. by setting a high number
  sourceRanges:    # Source IP addresses to allow in the firewall rules, any address can be inserted at this end as allowed range - '0.0.0.0/0'. For more than one entry comma must separate them and within quotes with each of it's corresponding name is required if you want exact match or else use a wildcard * for partial matches..
  targetTags:    # This field can be used to allow communication between resources, by default all outgoing traffic from the instance will go through this Firewall. In other words here we are specifying what kind of instances it should apply these rules too which is named in above 'instanceTemplateRef' or simply you may leave as empty for universal firewalls..
  allowed:   # Allows HTTP and HTTPS traffic only to the specified source ranges, if an IP address within this range has requested access then said request will be permitted. In other words it allows requests made by these specific networks (defined here via 'sourceRanges') through SSH DENY rule in GCE
  - values: ['0.0.0.0/0']   # Requests from which IP address the firewall is allowing access to this instance, all source range addresses could be added into it using * wildcard if multiple sources are needed separated by comma.. For example 'sourceRanges':['192.168.*','*'].
  targetInstances: true   # This enables communication between instances in the same network or on a specified subnetwork, must also use either instanceTemplateRef and regionalInstanceTemplateRef for this option to be enabled which can provide more control over traffic within your GCE environment.. If left unset then default behavior of Firewall rules is set (all outbound internet traffics are permitted).
  description: "Allow SSH access from any IP"   # This field gives a brief name or an identifier for the resource in human-friendly format. By providing this, it can be made easier to debug issues if something goes wrong within your GCE instances.. If left empty then no displayName will appear on UI and you should provide one otherwise .
